title: Oseon 5792
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses|Oseon}} {{CelestialBody |image= |name=Oseon 5792 |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]] |sector=[[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] |system=[[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]]''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' |coordinates=T-8 |xyz= |distance= |suns=[[Oseon sun]] |orbited= |position=5 {{C|located in the [[Fifth Belt]]}} |moons= |routes=*[[Cadma Conduit]] *[[Falko Run]] |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class= |diameter=Around 15 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity=*[[Artificial gravity/Legends|Artificial gravity]] *None in [[Bohhuah Mutdah's personal library]] |terrain=*[[Tree/Legends|Groves]] {{C|under [[Atmosphere/Legends|pressurized]] domes}} *Rolling lawns {{C|under pressurized domes}} |water=Small [[Water/Legends|lakes]] {{C|under pressurized domes}} |interest=*[[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah's]] personal [[Library/Legends|library]] *[[Garden/Legends|Gardens]] *[[Spaceport/Legends|Spaceport]] *Surface mansion complex |flora=*[[Grass/Legends|Grass]] {{C|under pressurized domes}} *[[Thorn tree/Legends|Thorn tree]] {{C|under pressurized domes}} |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies=Various, including [[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language=[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports=Library items {{C|[[Collector/Legends|collected]] by Bohhuah Mutdah}} |exports= |affiliation=Bohhuah Mutdah }} {{Quote|Nonetheless, I am required to instruct you to take this pair of law-enforcement officers to Oseon 5792, the home and estate of Bohhuah Mutdah, so that they may make their arrest.|Administrator Senior Lob Doluff, to Lando Calrissian on Captain Bassi Vobah and Waywa Fybot|}} '''Oseon 5792''', also known as '''Asteroid 5792''', was a fifteen-[[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]]-wide [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] located in the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. It served as the private estate of the trillionaire industrialist [[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah]], and it had a set of domed [[Garden/Legends|gardens]] on its surface. At the center of the asteroid, a [[Cave/Legends|chamber]] [[Bohhuah Mutdah's personal library|housed]] Mutdah's large [[Collector/Legends|collection]] of [[Library/Legends|library]] items. Sometime between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|Sorcerer of Tund]] [[Rokur Gepta]] planned to exact vengeance upon his nemesis, the [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. Gepta arranged for Calrissian to be invited to play a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] on the asteroid [[Oseon 6845]], where he was eventually placed under arrest. Gepta also made it so that a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] arrest order was issued for Mutdah, and pressured the [[Lob Doluff|Administrator Senior]] of the Oseon [[Star system/Legends|system]] to force Calrissian to participate in a corresponding sting operation on Oseon 5792. Calrissian; his [[Class Two multi-phasic robot|droid]] [[Pilot/Legends|copilot]], [[Vuffi Raa]]; and the [[Police officer/Legends|law enforcement officers]] [[Bassi Vobah]] and [[Waywa Fybot]] then all traveled to Oseon 5792 aboard Calrissian's [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. On the asteroid, Calrissian delivered a shipment of the illegal drug [[lesai]] to what he thought was Mutdah but was in reality a disguised Gepta. When Vobah attempted to place Gepta under arrest, Fybot—who had secretly been working for Mutdah—[[Death/Legends|killed]] his fellow [[Officer/Legends|officer]], following which Gepta in turn executed Fybot himself. The sorcerer then revealed his true identity to Calrissian and subsequently [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] him. Calrissian was saved when the [[Renatasian Confederation]], a group that was targeting Vuffi Raa and had followed the ''Millennium Falcon'' to Oseon 5792, [[Battle of Oseon|attacked]] the asteroid. Calrissian used the distraction to escape and returned to the ''Falcon'' and departed the asteroid. Gepta also escaped from Oseon 5792 before the Renatasian Confederation hurtled a [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|heavy cruiser]] [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]] at the asteroid, thereby destroying the worldlet. ==Description== ===A disk-shaped rock=== {{Quote|Does that catalog of yours give details on Mutdah's asteroid?''"
"''Fifty-seven ninety-two? Yes, Master, I—''"
"''Then it should give us some hints about the other asteroids around there; it's interested in the weird shape of this one. Let's get as close as we can, then pick our way, rock by rock, until we find the right one.|Lando Calrissian and Vuffi Raa|}} [[File:Oseon-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Oseon 5792 was located in the Oseon system.]] Oseon 5792, also referred to as Asteroid 5792,''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' was an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] located in the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]], a part of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Along with the rest of the Oseon [[Star system/Legends|system]], Oseon 5792 was connected by the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to the [[Arleen system/Legends|Arleen system]], while the [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route known as the [[Cadma Conduit]] linked it to the [[Erilnar system/Legends|Erilnar system]] and the [[Cadma sector/Legends|Cadma sector's]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} [[Dagelin Minor system/Legends|Dagelin Minor]] and [[Simbarc system/Legends|Simbarc systems]]. Also classified as a [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]], Oseon 5792 was a part of the [[Fifth Belt]], one of the seven wide [[Oseon asteroid belt|bands]] of asteroids that constituted the Oseon system and [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] its [[Oseon sun|sun]]. The asteroid was registered in a catalog listing the Oseon system's orbital bodies and was situated close to a flock of asteroids that were also well cataloged and easily identifiable. Oseon 5792 had the shape of a flattened disk, being approximately fifteen [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in diameter but less than three in thickness. The asteroid's negligible [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] was insufficient for maintaining a breathable [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], necessitating the use of [[Artificial gravity/Legends|artificial gravity]] for that purpose. In Oseon 5792's core, the gravitic pull was altogether nonexistent. ===The Flamewind of Oseon=== {{Quote|As you probably are aware, it is perilous in the extreme, and also illegal, for ships to travel from asteroid to asteroid in the Oseon during Flamewind.|Administrator Senior Lob Doluff, to Lando Calrissian|}} Once every [[local year]], Oseon 5792, along with every other orbital body in the Oseon system, was affected by a phenomenon known as the [[Flamewind of Oseon/Legends|Flamewind]]. Caused by the [[Solar flare/Legends|flares]] of the Oseon sun tearing excited vapor from the closest asteroids, the Flamewind manifested itself as fluorescent and multi-[[Color/Legends|colored]] bands of ionized [[Gas/Legends|gas]] millions of kilometers both long and wide that continuously shifted across the entire [[Spectrum/Legends|spectrum]] of visible light. Accompanying the visual display were frequent static discharges between the system's asteroids and a constant barrage of [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] and particles, such as [[Electron/Legends|electrons]]. As a result, navigational, [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]], and [[Life support/Legends|life-support]] equipment was damaged, electronic communications between the asteroids and with the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] were made impossible, and the perception and behavior of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings who were not protected by adequate [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]] was affected. ==History== ===Preparing the trap=== ====A trillionaire target==== {{Quote|The order for your arrest, sir, originated in the highest possible echelons. The very highest possible echelons.|Waywa Fybot, to Rokur Gepta in the disguise of Bohhuah Mutdah|}} [[File:The_Mindharp_of_Sharu.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Rokur Gepta sought vengeance upon Lando Calrissian (pictured).]] By [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] novel trilogy, including ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. Oseon 5792 had been inhabited for several [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]]. The asteroid was the private property of [[Bohhuah Mutdah/Legends|Bohhuah Mutdah]], a retired trillionaire industrialist and the wealthiest individual in the Oseon system, and it was the most luxurious of all the system's asteroid residences.{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}} At some point between 3 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]] and 2 BBY, the [[Sorcerers of Tund/Legends|sorcerer]] [[Rokur Gepta]] planned to exact vengeance upon the [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. To that end, Gepta arranged for Calrissian to be invited to play the [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] card game on the asteroid [[Oseon 6845]]. One [[Local day/Legends|night]] on Oseon 6845, the gambler was ambushed by a [[Kenow|member]] of the [[Renatasian Confederation]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''—a group devoted to exacting vengeance upon Calrissian's [[Class Two multi-phasic robot|droid]] [[Pilot/Legends|copilot]], [[Vuffi Raa]], whom it wrongfully held responsible for the [[Battle of Renatasia|subjugation]] of the [[Renatasia system/Legends|Renatasia system]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Acting in self-defense, Calrissian [[Death/Legends|killed]] the [[Renatasian]] and was subsequently placed under arrest. Around that [[Time/Legends|time]], Gepta arrived at Oseon 6845 on his personal [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]], the ''[[Wennis]]'', in pursuit of Calrissian. Gepta arranged for Imperial authorities to order the arrest of Mutdah, and subsequently, the Imperial undercover [[Spice/Legends|narcotics]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] [[Waywa Fybot]] was dispatched to the Oseon system to apprehend the trillionaire. ====Mission for a prisoner==== {{Quote|You will make transit to the next Belt inward, to the particular rock owned by Bohhuah Mutdah, and sell him the drug.|Administrator Senior Lob Doluff, to Lando Calrissian|}} [[File:Oseon6845-LCA.png|thumb|left|300px|Imprisoned on Oseon 6845 (pictured), Lando Calrissian was forced to participate in a sting operation on Oseon 5792.]] Eventually, [[Lob Doluff]], the [[Administrator Senior]] of the Oseon system, had Calrissian brought over from the local [[Prison/Legends|prison]] cell to his [[Administrator Senior's office|office]], where the former explained that, in lieu of the traditional Oseoni capital punishment for carrying a [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]], Calrissian would be forced to participate in a sting operation. The gambler was to [[Transport/Legends|transport]] Fybot and [[Oseon Peacekeeper]] Captain [[Bassi Vobah]] in his [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|starship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], to Oseon 5792—a trip that would normally only take slightly more than two [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] but was at that point extremely hazardous due to the Flamewind phenomenon that had commenced in the Oseon system. At his destination, Calrissian was to pose as a [[Courier/Legends|courier]] delivering Mutdah his scheduled supply of [[lesai]]—an illegal drug that was shipped annually to Mutdah under the cover of the Flamewind—allowing the two [[Police officer/Legends|law enforcement officers]] to arrest the trillionaire. In addition, Doluff, correctly suspecting Fybot of having been secretly ordered to kill Mutdah, explicitly ordered the agent to bring the industrialist back from Oseon 5792 to stand trial. The Administrator Senior also warned Vobah that she would be expected to leave the Oseon system directly from Oseon 5792 if she caused any unnecessary trouble for Calrissian. ====Departing for Oseon 5792==== {{Quote|This is Captain Calrissian speaking. Hope you two are thoroughly uncomfortable over there. We're off the ground and headed toward the Fifth Belt.|Lando Calrissian, to Captain Bassi Vobah and Waywa Fybot|}} [[File:MillenniumFalcon-FlamewindOfOseon.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The ''Millennium Falcon'', in which Lando Calrissian traveled to Oseon 5792]] The following day, the ''Millennium Falcon'', with both Fybot and Vobah aboard, left Oseon 6845, with Calrissian and Vuffi Raa being forced to engage on a [[Dead-reckoning program|pre-programmed]] course to Oseon 5792 due to the Flamewind affecting their [[Light freighter/Legends|ship's]] instrumentation. Eventually, Gepta, too, boarded his [[Rokur Gepta's starfighter|personal starfighter]] and departed Oseon 6845 for Oseon 5792, leaving the ''Wennis'' behind and instructing the vessel to rendezvous with him in the [[Tund system/Legends|Tund system]] after the Flamewind had passed. The [[Zero Squadron|squadron]] of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] making up the Renatasian Confederation, which had followed the ''Falcon'' from Oseon 6845, eventually caught up with the freighter and [[Space warfare/Legends|attacked]] it, which forced Calrissian to disengage his [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Computer/Legends|computer]]-controlled pre-[[Programmer/Legends|programmed]] flight toward Oseon 5792 in order to perform evasive maneuvers. The ''Falcon'' then briefly hid in a deep crevasse on a [[Unidentified Oseon system asteroid|small asteroid]]. ====A brief stop in the voyage==== {{Quote|My inclination—and if you think I'm joking, you're woefully deceived—is to handcuff the pair of you together until we get to 5792.|Lando Calrissian, to Captain Bassi Vobah and Waywa Fybot|}} Calrissian and Vuffi Raa made an unsuccessful attempt to discern from the ''Falcon''{{'s}} radiation-damaged [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]] the asteroid's exact location in the Oseon system. When the droid informed Calrissian of his similarly useless knowledge about the asteroid from the system's astronomical catalog, the captain, assuming that the catalog would have entries on asteroids in Oseon 5792's vicinity, decided that they would simply follow the pre-programmed course to Oseon 5792 as if they had not deviated from it due to the skirmish against the Renatasians and then attempt to correct for the error as they arrived closer to the target asteroid. When Calrissian was later discussing with Vobah and Fybot his suspicions that one of them had attempted to kill him while he had been repairing the ''Falcon'', the gambler attempted to order that the two [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|law enforcement]] [[Officer/Legends|officers]] [[Binders/Legends|handcuff]] themselves together for the duration of the voyage to Oseon 5792. At that moment, Vuffi Raa interrupted the conversation by summoning the captain to the ship's [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], for the Renatasian [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] had arrived in the vicinity of the ''Falcon''{{'s}} hiding place. Calrissian then enacted a plan that allowed the ''Falcon'' to evade the starfighters while leaving the refuge of the asteroid and continuing traveling toward Oseon 5792. ====The trillionaire and the sorcerer==== {{Quote|I and I alone arranged for that decadent leviathan to be harrassed by the government, then had him killed and took his place. All so I would be here when you arrived.|Rokur Gepta, on using Bohhuah Mutdah to entrap Lando Calrissian|}} [[File:RokurGeptaCloseup-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|300px|On Oseon 5792, Rokur Gepta (pictured) murdered Bohhuah Mutdah and assumed his identity.]] While the ''Falcon'' was still en route to Oseon 5792, Mutdah was observing a performance being carried out on a lawn in front of his mansion complex on the asteroid. More than three hundred individuals of various [[Species/Legends|species]], hired by Mutdah expressly for the performance, progressed through every exceedingly obscene permutation of activity possible for them, following the trillionaire's detailed instructions in doing so. Mutdah himself, due to his attitude of boredom toward life in general, was not impressed by either the performance or the colorful displays of the Flamewind visible through the dome that enclosed the garden that the mansion was situated in. Around the same time, while waiting for Calrissian to arrive on Oseon 5792, Gepta had entered hiding in a place of stifling concealment that isolated him from his [[Rokur Gepta's pets|pet]]. Eventually, the sorcerer, who was adept at crafting [[Mind trick/Legends|illusions]], murdered Mutdah and assumed his appearance. ====Approaching the destination==== {{Quote|Fifty-seven ninety-two coming up, Master. I believe it's that big blob over there on the right.|Vuffi Raa, to Lando Calrissian|}} Eventually, at the end of its pre-programmed course, the ''Millennium Falcon'' arrived in the relative vicinity of Oseon 5792. While the ship was still at a much greater distance from the asteroid than the range of the most popular starship detection systems, Calrissian made Fybot and Vobah hide themselves under the freighter's corridor decking as part of the preparations for their mission to arrest Mutdah. At a point where Vuffi Raa thought he could see Oseon 5792 in the form of a large blob against the [[Star/Legends|starfield]], the gambler also checked his [[Environment suit/Legends|vacuum suit]] and one of his [[Stingbeam/Legends|stingbeam]] [[Pistol/Legends|pistols]] as well as the package of lesai he was to deliver to Mutdah. Nearly twenty hours after the point at which Calrissian had been expected by Mutdah to arrive at Oseon 5792, when the ''Falcon'' had drifted to a distance of approximately hundred kilometers from the trillionaire's asteroid, the vessel was hailed by a cruising [[Picket ship/Legends|picket ship]]. The ship ordered the ''Falcon'' to stop for an inspection, but after Calrissian provided a secret password given to him, the gambler's vessel was provided with instructions for landing on Oseon 5792—a task which Calrissian, being both a relatively inexperienced ship-handler and nervous about the upcoming mission, delegated to Vuffi Raa. ===Calrissian on Oseon 5792=== ====Arrival==== {{Quote|Well, fellows, everybody here enjoying Flamewind? Where is everybody, by the way?|Lando Calrissian, to Bohhuah Mutdah's security guards|}} [[File:Lando-FlamewindCover.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Lando Calrissian around the time of his visit to Oseon 5792]] After the freighter made a gentle landing at Oseon 5792's [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]—which, despite appearing from an altitude of several kilometers to be a bustling facility, was by that point devoid of the presence of any organic or mechanical beings—Calrissian, while finishing suiting up, told Vuffi Raa that, considering the fact that he could potentially not return from the mission, he had logged the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] [[Manumission/Legends|manumission]] into the ''Falcon''{{'s}} memory system and also made him his legal heir. In return, Calrissian's copilot plainly made it known to him that, despite his master's orders to stay at the ship and depart the asteroid if the gambler did not return within eight hours' time, he would instead attempt to find Calrissian first. Calrissian then made Vobah, who was still concealed in the ship's corridor, turn over the sabacc winnings that had been confiscated from him after his arrest on Oseon 6845. After hiding the money aboard the ''Falcon'', Calrissian stepped out onto the spaceport's landing pad, from which he was rapidly escorted by a pair of [[Security guard/Legends|security guards]] to an elevator leading into the asteroid's interior. At the end of the ride, during which the gambler made an unsuccessful attempt at conversation with the guards, Calrissian was led into [[Bohhuah Mutdah's personal library|Mutdah's personal library]], where he met Gepta—still disguised as Mutdah. ====Arrest attempt gone wrong==== {{Quote|Bohhuah Mutdah, you are under arrest on the authority of the Administrator Senior of the Oseon System, for trafficking and use of illegal substances!|Captain Bassi Vobah, to a disguised Rokur Gepta|}} The security guards made Calrissian surrender his concealed pistols, after which the captain was allowed to exchange the shipment of lesai for the corresponding payment. At that moment, Vobah and Fybot, who had also left the ''Falcon'' and arrived at the center of Oseon 5792, used a [[Bomb/Legends|directional charge]] to blow up the [[Library/Legends|library's]] [[Pneumatic door|door]], killing the two guards in the process. Vobah then proclaimed that "Mutdah" was being placed under arrest, at which point Gepta gave a signal to Fybot—who was secretly working for whom he believed was still the trillionaire—causing the narcotics agent to promptly shoot Vobah dead. Fybot then gave a report to Gepta on the events leading up to the arrest attempt, also noting his belief from before he had made an unsuccessful murder attempt on Calrissian during the voyage from Oseon 6845 that he would be able to use by himself the ''Millennium Falcon'' in order to complete the trip to Oseon 5792. Gepta then, no longer seeing Fybot as a useful asset, used a tiny concealed pistol of his own to execute the agent. Inviting the gambler to light a pair of high-quality [[Cigarra/Legends|cigars]] for them both, Gepta then forced Calrissian to handcuff himself via pair of [[force shackles]] and finally revealed his true identity and form to his captive. ====Torture of Calrissian==== {{Quote|You are about to experience an agony so excruciating, so unprecedented in the history of intelligent life, that being one of its first experimental subjects is a privilege and a signal honor. You have had a sample of it: ''torture by chagrin''.|Rokur Gepta, to Lando Calrissian|}} [[File:LandoGepta.jpg|thumb|left|300px|On Oseon 5792, Lando Calrissian was tortured by Rokur Gepta.]] Subsequently, both Gepta and Calrissian moved from the library at Oseon 5792's center to the domed garden in which Mutdah had previously watched his performance. The performers were no longer present, and Calrissian, lacking any recollection of traveling from the library, had been cuffed to an upended table in the lawn. Gloating over his capture of Calrissian, Gepta subjected the gambler to the illusion technique known as [[Memory Walk|torture by chagrin]]. The technique aimed to induce agony in the latter by forced him to re-experience distorted memories from his life involving shame, humiliation, and fear. Approximately half an hour into the [[Torture/Legends|torture]] session, Gepta also revealed to Calrissian how he had adversely affected the gambler's life while plotting against him since before the latter had arrived in the Oseon system. At that point, the Renatasian Confederation squadron, which had also arrived at Oseon 5792, began [[Battle of Oseon|attacking]] the dome Gepta and Calrissian were in, eventually breaching it. Gepta then struggled against the pull of the resultant wind caused by the escaping atmosphere, and Calrissian used the distraction to free one of his [[Limb/Legends|hands]] from the force shackles and then jam a [[tinklewood]] rod he had brought along in his [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]] suit into Gepta's [[Eye/Legends|eye]]. The injured sorcerer was subsequently swept away by an intense breeze. ====Escaping an attack==== {{Quote|Let's get the devil out of here!|Lando Calrissian, to Vuffi Raa|}} [[File:VuffiNew.png|thumb|right|300px|The Renatasian Confederation attacked Oseon 5792 while targeting Vuffi Raa (pictured).]] As Calrissian fully liberated himself from his restraints, he encountered one of Vuffi Raa's autonomous tentacle-like limbs that had been disconnected from the droid's main body—located in the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]]—and sent to look for the captain. After the gambler briefly inspected the [[Unidentified electronic equipment|electronic equipment]] Gepta had used to assist with his illusion-casting, the pair, with Vuffi Raa's tentacle showing the way, quickly entered Mutdah's mansion, which was deserted at that point, and boarded an elevator that took them to the spaceport on Oseon 5792's other side. At the same time, the Renatasian Confederation continued their assault on the asteroid, causing it to shake and shudder as a result of the attacks. At the spaceport, Calrissian and Vuffi Raa's appendage both squeezed through the halfway-stuck door of the building containing the elevator entrance. Only a fraction of a [[Standard second|second]] later, the structure was destroyed as the Renatasian fighters carried out a [[bombing run]] on the spaceport. The captain and his droid boarded the ''Falcon'' and rapidly departed Oseon 5792, with Calrissian fighting off a pair of Renatasian craft by using the ship's [[AG-2G quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]]. Gepta, who ultimately lost his pet on the asteroid, also left aboard his personal fighter, heading to the rendezvous with the ''Wennis''. ====An asteroid destroyed==== {{Quote|A year later, I traveled to Oseon 5792, a planetoid that belonged to the trillionaire Bohhuah Mutdah, and was greeted by the impossibly corpulent Mutdah himself. At least that's who I ''thought'' was greeting me until my host transformed before my eyes into Rokur Gepta, who'd killed the real Mutdah. What happened next was most unpleasant.|Lando Calrissian, ''How to Succeed in Everything''|[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]}} [[File:StarcaveLando.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Lando Calrissian eventually recounted his experiences on Oseon 5792 in a memoir.]] The remaining fighters of the Renatasian Confederation, unaware that their quarry had already departed the asteroid and intent to eliminate Vuffi Raa at any cost, then slung the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser]] [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]] they had collectively been using for faster-than-light travel at Oseon 5792. The resultant explosion, which briefly outshone even the still-ongoing Flamewind, destroyed the asteroid, although both the ''Falcon'' and the fighter of [[Klyn Shanga]], leader of the Renatasian squadron, outran both the fireball and the ensuing shower of debris. The ''Falcon'' subsequently recovered Shanga from his wrecked fighter before departing the Oseon system. In [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], Calrissian briefly recounted his experiences on Oseon 5792 in his memoir, ''[[How to Succeed in Everything]]'', with the corresponding excerpt later being included in [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]]'s [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] compendium, ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]''. Calrissian's visit to the asteroid was also recorded in a [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] history [[The Essential Chronology|chronicle]] by the [[Historical Council]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]][[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] as well as its [[36 ABY]] [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|updated edition]] by the Council's incarnation under the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. By [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]],{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}} establishes that [[Ema Slake's estate]] was the most luxurious of the [[Oseon system/Legends|Oseon system]] [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] habitats thirty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Oseon|destruction]] of Oseon 5792, which—since ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', including that event, to between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]—translates to some point by [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]]. Oseon 5792's former status as the most lavish of the Oseon system asteroid residences had instead come to apply to the [[Ema Slake's estate|estate]] of the galaxy-renowned [[Musician/Legends|singer]] [[Ema Slake]] on the asteroid [[Oseon 5631]]. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|Bohhuah Mutdah is a retired industrialist, a trillionaire. His holdings in the Oseon are the largest in the system by a single individual, and it is possible that he is the wealthiest person in the civilized galaxy.|Waywa Fybot|}} Oseon 5792 served as the private estate of Bohhuah Mutdah. An obese [[Human/Legends|Human]] weighing more than three hundred [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]], he wore a large [[Rafa system/Legends|Rafa]] [[life-crystal]], a type of [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] that extended [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] lifespans, and had reached the age of more than one [[Standard century/Legends|hundred]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] by the time of his death.{{CSWECite|II|345-6|Mutdah, Bohhuah}} At that point, Mutdah had also not entered a genuine [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] gravitic field in a quarter of a century and, due to a combination of his immense wealth and obesity, had not used his own hands for any purpose for the span of five years. The asteroid was also inhabited by Mutdah's servants and staff. For protection from potential [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] among his employees, on at least one occasion Mutdah was surrounded by a [[Force field/Legends|force field]]. Mutdah's staff included the elderly servant [[Ekisp]] and two guards, one of which held the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of a [[Sergeant/Legends|sergeant]]. Each of the pair of guards spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] and wore a set of heavy body [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and stylish livery at the same time. The asteroid's security forces made use of at least one brand-new, heavily armed, starfighter-sized ship, the weaponry of which was comparable to that of a light freighter. ==Locations== ===Gardens=== One of the sides of Oseon 5792 was dotted with high domes of crystalline [[Plastic/Legends|plastic]] and [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] that together formed a mythological [[Garden/Legends|garden]] consisting of rolling lawns, small [[Water/Legends|lakes]], and occasional groves of [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. The pressurized domes were necessary in order to maintain a breathable atmosphere since the pull of the artificial gravity on that side of the asteroid was insufficient for that purpose. Since Lando Calrissian first observed the garden side of Oseon 5792, the asteroid resembled to him an island floating in [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. One of the domed gardens—which was heavily shielded, situated at the edge of Oseon 5792's garden side, and large enough to stretch to the horizon from the point of view of an observer inside it—contained a large surface mansion complex that resembled an old-fashioned palace. A wide, rich lawn stretched in front of the building and featured a [[Diet/Legends|picnic]] table made out of a synthetic [[Marble/Legends|marble]]. A grove of [[Thorn tree/Legends|thorn trees]] was situated near the lawn, and at one point Bohhuah Mutdah spent time lounging in a [[gel-filled recliner]] in front of the expanse of [[Grass/Legends|grass]]. ===Library=== {{Main|Bohhuah Mutdah's personal library}} {{Quote|There is a box of excellent cigars in the top drawer of the end table. Would you kindly remove two of them, light them with the lighter you will also discover there, give one of them to me, and enjoy the other yourself?|Rokur Gepta, to Lando Calrissian while disguised as Bohhuah Mutdah|}} Similar to nearly every other Oseon system asteroid inhabited by Humans, over the duration of its occupation the interior of Oseon 5792 was steadily honeycombed and outfitted with storerooms, living quarters, utility areas, and spaces for other uses. At the center of the asteroid was a spherical [[Cave/Legends|cavern]] half a kilometer in diameter. The titanic chamber was set up to house Mutdah's personal library, with the walls of the room lined with items the trillionaire had [[Collector/Legends|collected]]—every known variety of written or other records, including printed and [[Writing/Legends|handwritten]] works, analog and digital data storage devices, tablets, stones, [[Bone/Legends|bones]], [[Leather/Legends|hides]], and clumps of knotted rope. Hundreds of fluorescent [[Monofilament cable|monofilament threads]] formed a mesh that drifted through the zero-gravity chamber and served as a means of transportation across the room. In the center of the library, a small table, used for tucking under an elastic band a book Mutdah's servant Ekisp was reading to his master, floated in mid-air. A drawer in the table also contained a box of high-quality cigars. ===Spaceport=== {{Quote|What did they say?''"
"''That we're expected and should set down on the small field opposite the surface mansion complex—but not to try any dirty tricks.|Lando Calrissian and Vuffi Raa|}} The side of Oseon 5792 located opposite the one with the domed gardens served as a miniature spaceport. The facility, which Lando Calrissian found to be impressive, consisted of a starship landing field that stretched across the entire side of the asteroid and was ringed at its edges with heavy armament. The spaceport was cluttered with a very large motley [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of spacecraft, including many small inter-asteroidal vessels, that together resembled a hobbyist's collection. When the ''Millennium Falcon'' visited Oseon 5792, it was guided to land in a small berth designated with a pulsing beacon. A [[Ferrocrete/Legends|ferrocrete]] apron connected the parking spot to a service building made of corrugated plastic. Inside the building, an industrial-grade elevator with a pneumatic door led into the asteroid's interior, reaching the entrance of Mutdah's library at Oseon 5792's center before traveling to the mansion complex that was located on the opposite of the asteroid from the ''Falcon''{{'s}} landing berth. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Flamewind_of_Oseon_Cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Oseon 5792 was introduced in ''Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon''.]] Oseon 5792 appeared in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', the [[1983]] second entry in [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels by [[L. Neil Smith]]. The [[2005]] reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' introduced the alternate name "Asteroid 5792." "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed the Oseon system, and therefore Oseon 5792, in the [[Zebitrope sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}} it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.{{UnlicensedStatus}} The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the Oseon system was situated in grid square T-8 as part of the Centrality. ==Appearances== *''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{GamerCite|5|A Campaign Guide to the Centrality}} *{{FactFile|29|CAL 1-6|Lando Calrissian}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{1stID|Asteroid 5792}} *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *{{CSWECite|II||Mutdah, Bohhuah}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Oseon 5792 |nl=Oseon 5792 }} [[Category:Destroyed asteroids]] [[Category:Oseon system locations]] [[Category:Planetoids in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Planetoids in the Slice]]
